revenue.name: Total Revenue
revenue.description: Total sales revenue from all completed orders
revenue.sql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(line_total), 0) as revenue FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed' AND company_id = :company_id

gross_profit.name: Gross Profit
gross_profit.description: Revenue minus cost of goods sold
gross_profit.sql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(gross_profit), 0) as gross_profit FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed' AND company_id = :company_id

net_profit.name: Net Profit
net_profit.description: Revenue minus all expenses (COGS + operating expenses)
net_profit.sql: WITH revenue AS ( SELECT COALESCE(SUM(line_total), 0) as total_revenue FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed' AND company_id = :company_id ), cogs AS ( SELECT COALESCE(SUM(quantity * cost_price), 0) as total_cogs FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed' AND company_id = :company_id ), operating_expenses AS ( SELECT COALESCE(SUM(amount), 0) as total_expenses FROM fact_expenses WHERE company_id = :company_id ) SELECT (total_revenue - total_cogs - total_expenses) as net_profit FROM revenue, cogs, operating_expenses

cash_runway.name: Cash Runway
cash_runway.description: Number of months until cash runs out at current burn rate
cash_runway.sql: WITH monthly_burn AS ( SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(date AS DATE)) as month, SUM(amount) as monthly_expenses FROM fact_expenses WHERE company_id = :company_id GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(date AS DATE)) ORDER BY month DESC LIMIT 3 ), avg_monthly_burn AS ( SELECT AVG(monthly_expenses) as avg_burn FROM monthly_burn ), current_cash AS ( SELECT signed_amount as cash_balance FROM fact_cash_flow WHERE company_id = :company_id ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 1 ) SELECT ROUND(CAST(cash_balance / NULLIF(avg_burn, 0) AS numeric), 1) as cash_runway_months FROM current_cash, avg_monthly_burn

top_products.name: Top Selling Products
top_products.sql: SELECT dp.product_name, dp.category, SUM(fs.line_total) as revenue, SUM(fs.quantity) as units_sold FROM fact_sales fs JOIN dim_products dp ON fs.product_id = dp.product_id WHERE fs.status = 'completed' AND fs.company_id = :company_id GROUP BY dp.product_id, dp.product_name, dp.category ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10

marketing_roi.name: Marketing ROI
marketing_roi.description: Return on investment for marketing campaigns
marketing_roi.sql: SELECT dc.campaign_name, dc.platform, SUM(fm.spend) as total_spend, SUM(fm.revenue_attributed) as attributed_revenue, ((SUM(fm.revenue_attributed) - SUM(fm.spend)) / NULLIF(SUM(fm.spend), 0)) * 100 as roi_percentage FROM fact_marketing fm JOIN dim_campaigns dc ON fm.campaign_id = dc.campaign_id WHERE fm.company_id = :company_id GROUP BY dc.campaign_id, dc.campaign_name, dc.platform HAVING SUM(fm.spend) > 0 ORDER BY roi_percentage DESC

avg_order_value.name: Average Order Value
avg_order_value.sql: SELECT AVG(line_total) as avg_order_value FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed' AND company_id = :company_id

revenue_by_segment.name: Revenue by Customer Segment
revenue_by_segment.description: Total revenue broken down by customer segment (Retail, SME, Corporate)
revenue_by_segment.sql: SELECT dc.segment, SUM(fs.line_total) as total_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT fs.customer_id) as customers, ROUND(CAST(AVG(fs.line_total) AS numeric), 2) as avg_order_value FROM fact_sales fs JOIN dim_customers dc ON fs.customer_id = dc.customer_id WHERE fs.status = 'completed' AND fs.company_id = :company_id GROUP BY dc.segment ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

revenue_by_channel.name: Revenue by Sales Channel
revenue_by_channel.description: Total revenue broken down by sales channel
revenue_by_channel.sql: SELECT channel, SUM(line_total) as total_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) as total_orders FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed' AND company_id = :company_id GROUP BY channel ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

revenue_by_city.name: Revenue by City
revenue_by_city.description: Total revenue and orders broken down by customer city
revenue_by_city.sql: SELECT dc.city, SUM(fs.line_total) as total_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT fs.order_id) as total_orders FROM fact_sales fs JOIN dim_customers dc ON fs.customer_id = dc.customer_id WHERE fs.status = 'completed' AND fs.company_id = :company_id GROUP BY dc.city ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

expense_breakdown.name: Expense Breakdown by Category
expense_breakdown.sql: SELECT expense_category, SUM(amount) as total_amount, COUNT(*) as num_entries FROM fact_expenses WHERE company_id = :company_id GROUP BY expense_category ORDER BY total_amount DESC

top_customers.name: Top Customers by Revenue
top_customers.description: Customers ranked by total revenue generated
top_customers.sql: SELECT dc.full_name, dc.segment, dc.city, SUM(fs.line_total) as total_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT fs.order_id) as total_orders FROM fact_sales fs JOIN dim_customers dc ON fs.customer_id = dc.customer_id WHERE fs.status = 'completed' AND fs.company_id = :company_id GROUP BY dc.customer_id, dc.full_name, dc.segment, dc.city ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 10

orders_by_city.name: Orders by City
orders_by_city.description: Number of orders and revenue from each city
orders_by_city.sql: SELECT dc.city, COUNT(DISTINCT fs.order_id) as num_orders, SUM(fs.line_total) as total_revenue FROM fact_sales fs JOIN dim_customers dc ON fs.customer_id = dc.customer_id WHERE fs.status = 'completed' AND fs.company_id = :company_id GROUP BY dc.city ORDER BY num_orders DESC

worst_campaign.name: Worst Performing Campaigns
worst_campaign.description: Campaigns ranked by lowest ROI (wasting money, negative returns)
worst_campaign.sql: SELECT dc.campaign_name, dc.platform, SUM(fm.spend) as total_spend, SUM(fm.revenue_attributed) as attributed_revenue, ((SUM(fm.revenue_attributed) - SUM(fm.spend)) / NULLIF(SUM(fm.spend), 0)) * 100 as roi_percentage FROM fact_marketing fm JOIN dim_campaigns dc ON fm.campaign_id = dc.campaign_id WHERE fm.company_id = :company_id GROUP BY dc.campaign_id, dc.campaign_name, dc.platform HAVING SUM(fm.spend) > 0 ORDER BY roi_percentage ASC

customer_retention_rate.name: Customer Retention Rate
customer_retention_rate.description: Percentage of customers who made more than one purchase
customer_retention_rate.sql: SELECT ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN order_count > 1 THEN customer_id END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id), 0), 1) AS customer_retention_rate FROM (SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed' AND company_id = :company_id GROUP BY customer_id) t

churn_risk.name: Churn Risk Customers
churn_risk.description: Customers with a churn risk score above 0.7 (high risk of leaving)
churn_risk.sql: SELECT dc.full_name, dc.segment, ROUND(CAST(cm.churn_risk_score AS numeric), 3) AS churn_risk_score, EXTRACT(DAY FROM NOW() - MAX(fs.order_date))::int AS last_order_days_ago FROM dim_customers dc JOIN fact_sales fs ON fs.customer_id = dc.customer_id LEFT JOIN customer_metrics cm ON cm.customer_id = dc.customer_id WHERE dc.company_id = :company_id AND cm.churn_risk_score > 0.7 GROUP BY dc.customer_id, dc.full_name, dc.segment, cm.churn_risk_score ORDER BY cm.churn_risk_score DESC LIMIT 10

service_revenue.name: Total Service Revenue
service_revenue.description: Total revenue from completed service bookings
service_revenue.sql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(line_total), 0) AS service_revenue FROM fact_service_bookings WHERE status = 'completed' AND company_id = :company_id

top_services.name: Top Services by Revenue
top_services.description: Services ranked by total revenue from bookings
top_services.sql: SELECT ds.service_name, ds.category, SUM(fsb.sessions) AS total_sessions, SUM(fsb.line_total) AS revenue FROM fact_service_bookings fsb JOIN dim_services ds ON fsb.service_id = ds.service_id WHERE fsb.status = 'completed' AND fsb.company_id = :company_id GROUP BY ds.service_id, ds.service_name, ds.category ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10

service_booking_count.name: Total Service Bookings
service_booking_count.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_bookings FROM fact_service_bookings WHERE status = 'completed' AND company_id = :company_id

service_revenue_by_category.name: Service Revenue by Category
service_revenue_by_category.description: Total service revenue grouped by service category
service_revenue_by_category.sql: SELECT ds.category, SUM(fsb.line_total) AS revenue, COUNT(*) AS bookings FROM fact_service_bookings fsb JOIN dim_services ds ON fsb.service_id = ds.service_id WHERE fsb.status = 'completed' AND fsb.company_id = :company_id GROUP BY ds.category ORDER BY revenue DESC
